methods.description: This method involves measuring the major element compositions of glass samples from various eruptions using an electron microprobe. The purpose is to compare the glass compositions of different samples to understand the characteristics of the eruptions.

steps.description: Collect glass samples from F-02, Cha1, Mic2, and Chaitén 2008 eruptions.
steps.searchable_summary: collect glass samples from specified eruptions

steps.description: Prepare samples by polishing and mounting them in resin.
steps.searchable_summary: prepare samples by polishing and resin mounting

steps.description: Measure major element compositions using an electron microprobe with a 5-micron spot size, 4 nA beam current, and 15 kV voltage.
steps.searchable_summary: measure major element compositions with electron microprobe

steps.description: Exclude analyses of lithic grains due to mixed appearance and potential contamination from older eruptions.
